OUR MAYOR ON HIS TOWN

A BLUFF

pages.raw_text:
j. M. Bum, attorney, and A. Beam
or, superintendent of the Northern
Pacific railway. appeared before the
finance committee of the city council
yesterday afternoon to spring a
scheme whereby it is expected the
city can be persuaded to drop the
condemnation proceedings for the
opening of Browne street across the
railroad tracks.


The railroad people gave a large
talk about how the company intended
to put in subways for every street
between Divisian and Monroe street
and west Of Monroe street lower the
tracks.


This plan was put forward as a
cause for stopping the condemnation
proceedings, but when the railroad
representatives were asked the direct
question when these plans would be
executed the answer was that the
plans were only proposed and there
was no definite agreement as to
whether they would be used.


To the committee this looked too
much like the regulation railroad
bluff which has been used with more
or less success upon Spokane mer-
chants for years. For this reason
no instructions were given to dis
miss the proceedings. The corpora
tion counsel did agree, however, to
delay the trial of the condemnation
proceedings until the middle Of No
vember.


IT'S just a bluff, pure and simple"
said one councilman after the meet
ing. "The railroad company has no
intention at this time of changing
the tracks"
